content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Remediated the panel-backend parsing regression by teaching `PanelBackendSpec::from_str` to split `family/model` only on `/` characters that occur outside parentheses, so legacy `backend(model)` entries like `?openrouter(openai/gpt-5.4)` continue to parse while the new inline `family/model` syntax still works. Added regression coverage for direct parsing, serde acceptance/normalization of legacy parenthesized OpenRouter values, and `config set` acceptance plus effective-config display for legacy panel backend entries.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Updated `src/shared/domain.rs` so panel backend parsing recognizes `family/model` only when the slash is outside parentheses, preserving legacy `backend(model)` panel specs with slash-containing model ids.
2. [Completed] Added regression tests covering legacy parenthesized panel backend parsing and serde normalization for OpenRouter values that include `/` inside the model spec.
3. [Completed] Added config validation coverage proving `EffectiveConfig::set` accepts legacy parenthesized panel backend entries and that effective config display normalizes them to the canonical `family/model` form.
4. [Completed] Ran validation commands for the amended change set and confirmed formatting and test coverage status.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test --test unit parenthesized_model_overrides` passed, covering the new legacy-parse and serde regression tests.
- `cargo test --test unit config_set_accepts_legacy_parenthesized_panel_backend_model_overrides` passed.
- `cargo test` passed on rerun after one transient unrelated checkpoint-test failure; the successful rerun completed with all test suites passing.
- Verified by inspection that `apply_string_list` validation in `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs` reuses `PanelBackendSpec::from_str`, so the parser fix restores `config set` acceptance for legacy panel specs without additional code changes there.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` was run and failed only on unrelated existing repo-wide warnings, not on the panel-backend parser or the new regression tests.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` still fails on pre-existing repo-wide issues outside this change set, including dead code in `src/cli/run.rs`, unrelated clippy findings in `src/adapters/fs.rs`, multiple `too_many_arguments` findings in milestone services, and existing `items_after_test_module` violations.
